explanations.3: The !important flag forces this style to override any conflicting styles, which is sometimes necessary but can make CSS harder to maintain.
explanations.28: This creates a custom CSS variable called --chevron-rotation that can be changed by other rules to rotate the chevron icon.
explanations.35: This selector targets elements with aria-expanded="true", which is an accessibility attribute that indicates whether a collapsible section is open or closed.
explanations.52: This uses the custom variable defined on line 28 to rotate the chevron; the variable gets updated when the element's aria-expanded state changes.
explanations.73: The bracket notation [class$="_icon"] selects any element whose class name ends with "_icon", so it matches .copy_icon, .alert_icon, etc.
explanations.81: The bracket notation [class^="variant_"] selects any element whose class name starts with "variant_", so it matches .variant_python, .variant_bash, etc.
explanations.128: This uses a fallback value (#282c34) in case the CSS variable --prism-background-color isn't defined, so the code block still has a dark background.
explanations.148: The white-space: pre property preserves all whitespace and line breaks exactly as written, which is essential for displaying code.
explanations.156: The flex-shrink: 0 prevents this placeholder from shrinking smaller than its natural size, keeping it at a consistent width.
explanations.202: The calc() function adds 6px to 100% of the tooltip's height, pushing the tooltip up above the badge with a small gap.
explanations.217: The pointer-events: none makes the tooltip invisible to the mouse, so clicks pass through it to elements below.
explanations.221: The ::before pseudo-element creates a small arrow shape that points downward from the tooltip toward the badge button.
